sentiment.label: risk_on
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è migliorato grazie alle notizie di possibili negoziati tra USA e Iran per porre fine al conflitto. Il VIX è sceso del 17.51% a 25.25, mentre l'S&P 500 è salito del 2.91% a 6528.52. Il prezzo del petrolio è sceso del 15.10% a $100.48, riducendo le preoccupazioni sull'inflazione.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment has improved due to news of possible negotiations between the US and Iran to end the conflict. The VIX dropped 17.51% to 25.25, while the S&P 500 rose 2.91% to 6528.52. Oil prices fell 15.10% to $100.48, reducing inflation concerns.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario sta registrando una ripresa grazie alle speranze di una risoluzione del conflitto tra USA e Iran. L'indice DXY è sceso dello 0.35% a 99.60, mentre l'euro/dollaro è salito dell'1.18% a 1.15. Il prezzo dell'oro è aumentato del 2.17% a $4748.30. Il mercato obbligazionario sta vedendo una riduzione dei rendimenti, con il TLT che è sceso dello 0.10% a 86.69.
market_impact_summary.en: The stock market is recovering due to hopes of a resolution to the US-Iran conflict. The DXY index fell 0.35% to 99.60, while the euro/dollar rose 1.18% to 1.15. Gold prices increased 2.17% to $4748.30. The bond market is seeing reduced yields, with TLT down 0.10% to 86.69.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno e benvenuti a questo briefing finanziario. Iniziamo la nostra analisi dei mercati tradizionali e della macroeconomia globale.

I mercati asiatici hanno chiuso la giornata con un sentiment positivo. Il Nikkei giapponese ha registrato una crescita del 5.24%, chiudendo a 53,740 punti. Anche lo Shanghai ha mostrato un aumento del 1.38%, raggiungendo 3945.40 punti. Il sentiment globale è di risk-on, con gli investitori che sembrano ottimisti riguardo alle prospettive economiche.

Passando ai mercati occidentali, l'S&P 500 ha registrato una crescita significativa del 2.91%, raggiungendo 6528.52 punti. Il Dollar Index (DXY) ha mostrato un calo del 0.35%, attestandosi a 99.60 punti. Il VIX, indice di volatilità, è sceso del 17.51% a 25.25 punti. L'oro ha guadagnato il 2.17%, raggiungendo $4748.30 l'oncia. Il prezzo del petrolio Brent è sceso del 15.10% a $100.48 al barile. I tassi di interesse sono stati influenzati da questi movimenti, con il tasso decennale USA che è sceso dello 0.71% al 4.31%.

Sul fronte geopolitico, si segnalano diverse tensioni a livello globale. L'inflazione nell'Eurozona è salita al 2.5% a marzo, in parte a causa dell'impatto del conflitto in Iran. La Banca Centrale Europea (BCE) sta monitorando la situazione in vista della sua prossima riunione. Negli Usa, i dati macroeconomici recenti mostrano segnali contrastanti, con un tasso di disoccupazione in aumento al 4.4%. La Federal Reserve sembra mantenere una posizione cauta sui tassi d'interesse, con il tasso Fed Funds stabile al 3.64%.

Concludiamo qui la nostra analisi dei mercati tradizionali e della macroeconomia globale. Tra poco, passeremo a esaminare il comparto digitale, con un focus su Bitcoin e le altre criptovalute.

Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali. Iniziamo con un deep dive su Bitcoin. La criptovaluta ha registrato un aumento del 2.45%, raggiungendo il valore di $69,101. Gli ETF su Bitcoin hanno visto un afflusso di $69.4M, segno di un interesse ancora presente nel mercato. 

  Spostando l'attenzione sulle altcoin, Ethereum è salita del 4.37% a $2,151, mentre Solana ha guadagnato l'1.43% a $84.42. Binance Coin, invece, ha registrato un aumento del 1.24% a $619.32.

  Analizzando il sentiment del mercato, l'indice Fear & Greed è sceso a 8, classificandosi come Estrema Paura. Questo livello di paura estrema potrebbe indicare una possibile reazione a breve termine. Il contesto macroeconomico, con dati non recenti che mostrano un'inflazione in calo e un mercato del lavoro in rallentamento, potrebbe influenzare positivamente il mercato delle criptovalute nel lungo termine.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is positive today, with investors focusing on the latest economic data. The US Non-Farm Payrolls report showed a decline of 92,000 jobs, but this was largely expected. Meanwhile, the Core CPI Year over Year rate remains steady at 2.7%.

In traditional markets, equities are performing well. The S&P 500 closed at 6528.52, up 2.91% overnight. The Euro to US Dollar exchange rate is 1.15, up 1.18%. The Dollar Index is at 99.60, down 0.35%. The 10-year US Treasury yield is at 4.31%, down 0.71%. Commodities are also seeing significant moves, with Gold at 4748.30 dollars per ounce, up 2.17%, and Brent crude at 100.48 dollars per barrel, down 15.10%.

Geopolitically, tensions in the Middle East are having an impact on oil prices and inflation. The European Central Bank is set to announce its next monetary policy decision on April 17. The ECB's three-pronged monetary strategy is being closely watched, and there are concerns about the impact of geopolitical turmoil on central banking. In the Eurozone, inflation jumped to 2.5% in March, reflecting the impact of the Iran conflict. The ECB's Olli Rehn discussed how to navigate these challenges in a European context.

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

Starting with Bitcoin, we see a price increase of 2.45% to $69,101, with the BTC ETF daily net inflow coming in at $69.4M. From a technical standpoint, Bitcoin has been showing a positive trend, but it's essential to keep an eye on the overall market sentiment.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the top altcoins. Ethereum is up 4.37% to $2,151, while Solana and Binance Coin are also in the green, with gains of 1.43% and 1.24%, respectively. These movements suggest a relatively stable and positive trend across the altcoin space.

Now, looking at market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 8, indicating extreme fear. This is a crucial indicator to monitor, as it can often signal potential buying opportunities. Historically, periods of extreme fear have been followed by significant price movements.

In terms of market correlation, it's worth noting that the cryptocurrency market is often influenced by broader macroeconomic factors. However, with the current index analysis, we see that the market is primarily driven by internal dynamics. 

Overall, the cryptocurrency market is showing signs of resilience, with Bitcoin and altcoins experiencing price increases. However, it's essential to remain cautious and monitor market sentiment, as the Fear & Greed Index suggests that we may still be in a period of uncertainty.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you soon.

articles.title: The Cost of Hesitation: Why “Finishing the Mission” is Imperative in Iran
articles.snippet: Editor’s Note: War on the Rocks is running “for” and “against” articles on the war with Iran by legislators with military backgrounds. You can find the other article here.The view from a C-130 during a combat mission in the Middle East provides clarity that is often lost in the sanitized briefing rooms of Washington. As an Air Force medical crew director, I have looked into the eyes of young servicemembers being medevacked out of theater, and I lived through what decades of strategic inconsistency asks of our warfighters. As a board-certified psychiatric mental health nurse practitioner, I am equally attuned The post The Cost of Hesitation: Why “Finishing the Mission” is Imperative in Iran appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: Holding Water Hostage
articles.snippet: Parts of the Middle East — especially around the Persian Gulf — rely on desalinated water. Worst-case scenarios for war in the region have often included attacks on desalination facilities, and the war with Iran that began on Feb. 28 has raised the potential for such a scenario. So far, there have been reports that a U.S. strike on Qeshm Island damaged an Iranian desalination plant and that an Iranian drone had hit a desalination plant in Bahrain. On March 30, an attack damaged a building at a power and desalination site in Kuwait. Iran has warned that it could The post Holding Water Hostage appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: The Trump Administration Had No Plan for the War with Iran
articles.snippet: Editor’s Note: War on the Rocks is running “for” and “against” articles on the war with Iran by legislators with military backgrounds. You can find the other article here.21 years ago, I was a Marine infantryman in Anbar province, western Iraq, carrying so much gear that I could barely feel my legs. My job was to go town to town, search houses, and try to stop insurgents before they stopped us. Every day, someone was trying to kill us — improvised explosive devices buried in the roads, mortars in the middle of the night, and random rocket propelled grenades fired The post The Trump Administration Had No Plan for the War with Iran appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: OPEC Output Plunges by 7 Million Bpd as War Chokes Supply
articles.snippet: Crude oil production by OPEC members dropped by 7.2 million barrels daily last month, according to a Reuters survey using data from platform LSEG and analytics information providers, including Kpler, the publication reported. The biggest production cuts were made by Kuwait, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates, and Saudi Arabia, for a total OPEC output of 21.57 million barrels daily for March. This is the lowest OPEC production rate since June 2020, Reuters noted in its report. Venezuela and Nigeria were the only OPEC members that saw their oil production…
articles.title_it: Produzione OPEC in picchiata a causa della guerra
articles.title_en: OPEC output plunges due to war
articles.summary_it: La produzione di petrolio dell'OPEC è scesa di 7.2 milioni di barili al giorno a causa della guerra.
articles.summary_en: OPEC's oil production dropped by 7.2 million barrels per day due to the war.

articles.title: $150 Oil Could Tip World Economy Into Recession
articles.snippet: The world economy is heading for a “rare” recession in the middle of this year as a prolonged war seems likely amid the prospect of US troops heading to the Middle East. Economists have warned that activity will fall in the middle of the year if oil prices surge to $150 per barrel and remain there for a period of four months. Oil prices continued to climb higher on Monday as the Brent Crude benchmark raced past the $116 per barrel mark amid mixed messages between the US, Israel and Iran on the state of the war. It…

articles.title: India Boosts Diesel Exports to Southeast Asia to 7-Year High
articles.snippet: Tightening Asian fuel markets and Indian refiners’ thirst for Russian crude have resulted in the highest Indian diesel exports to Southeast Asia in seven and a half years in March, Reuters reported on Tuesday, citing trade sources and data from analytics firm Kpler. As the Middle East war roils Asian fuel markets and prompted many countries in Asia, including China, to impose fuel export bans, Indian refiners are taking advantage of the higher premiums they get to send their fuel to Southeast Asia, which began struggling with fuel shortages.…

articles.title: U.S. Senators Unveil ‘Mined in America Act’ to Reshore BTC Mining, Codify Bitcoin Strategic Reserve
articles.snippet: Bitcoin Magazine U.S. Senators Unveil ‘Mined in America Act’ to Reshore BTC Mining, Codify Bitcoin Strategic Reserve Senators Bill Cassidy and Cynthia Lummis introduced the “Mined in America Act” to reshore U.S. bitcoin mining, reduce reliance on Chinese hardware, and codify a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve into law. This post U.S. Senators Unveil ‘Mined in America Act’ to Reshore BTC Mining, Codify Bitcoin Strategic Reserve first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

articles.title: India’s Aviation Market Rocked by Wild Jet Fuel Price Swings
articles.snippet: Indian Oil Corp. earlier today raised jet fuel prices for local airlines significantly, before cutting them sharply hours later, media are reporting, as the world’s third-largest importer of crude grapples with the fallout of the Middle East war. Jet fuel prices in India surged twofold earlier in the day, the Indian Express reported, hitting an all-time high of 207,341 rupees, equal to almost $2,200 per kilolitre in New Delhi, as the country’s largest oil company sought to manage the impact of oil import disruptions. Hours later, however,…

articles.title: Guyana's Oil Boom Will Boost Energy Security in the Americas
articles.snippet: Guyana, once among South America's most impoverished nations, is experiencing an epic oil boom. In a mere four years, the country of less than one million went from first discovery to first oil and is now lifting over 900,000 barrels per day. There are signs of significant production growth ahead. This is occurring at a crucial time for a world experiencing an energy crisis after oil spiked to well-over $110 per barrel after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for U.S. and Israeli airstrikes. Guyana's oil boom is significantly reducing…

articles.title: Bitcoin hits $68K but BTC futures, macro data show traders remain bearish
articles.snippet: Bitcoin rallied to $68,000 as markets responded positively to the prospect of the US and Israel-Iran war ending, but data shows futures traders are not convinced.
articles.title_it: Bitcoin hits $68K but BTC futures, macro data show traders remain bearish
articles.title_en: Bitcoin hits $68K but BTC futures, macro data show traders remain bearish
articles.summary_it: Bitcoin rallied to $68,000 as markets responded positively to the prospect of the US and Israel-Iran war ending, but data shows futures traders are not convinced.
articles.summary_en: Bitcoin rallied to $68,000 as markets responded positively to the prospect of the US and Israel-Iran war ending, but data shows futures traders are not convinced.
